0.2.2.title: Tranlsations
notes: Tranlsations

0.1.16.title: Version tagging + auto-rebuild on mismatch
notes: Git commit and tag on every build (build: vX.Y.Z) — rollback via git checkout tag
Stale container detection: EXPECTED_BUILD_ID passed to containers; mismatch writes rebuild IPC task
Host handles rebuild IPC task by spawning build.sh detached — auto-recovers without manual intervention
Changelog auto-generated from git commits on each build — tag and changelog always in sync
Path constants extracted in ollama-runner/index.ts — no more inline magic strings
keep_alive set to 0.1m (6s) for all specialist models; coordinator and secretary pinned at -1

0.1.15.title: SearXNG self-hosted search + voice transcription tests
notes: Web search replaced with local SearXNG container (privacy-first, no external tracking, port 8888)
SearXNG auto-starts in start.sh with secret key generated on first run
SEARXNG_HOST env var passed to all Ollama runner containers
Voice transcription test suite: 13 unit tests covering success, failure, timeout, cleanup
Telegram channel voice tests: 10 integration tests for transcript routing and fallback
SearXNG settings: metrics, limiter, image proxy, and analytics all disabled

0.1.14.title: Needs-web classification + secretary intelligence
notes: needs_web field in MessageClassification — secretary signals when live data required
Secretary draft suppressed when needs_web is true — prevents stale answers on live queries
Coordinator receives '[Live data required — call web_search before answering]' hint when needsWeb
Secretary feedback loop: coordinator grades routing quality after every response
Grades stored per-group in .secretary-feedback.json, last 5 corrections injected into classify prompt
47-test classify suite covering routing, escalation, secretary review, and all fallback paths

0.1.9.title: Fresh off the press
notes: v0.1.5: Secretary intelligence, web access, scheduling, memory, and testing
v0.0.75: Artist/Cinematographer, model routing, and team identity
Cleanup
Video support
Optimize
Optimize
Commit
Auto start video processors
resolve: keep local changes over origin/main in merge conflict
ComfyUI

0.1.5.title: needs_web flag — secretary signals live-data questions, coordinator primed to search immediately
notes: MessageClassification gains needsWeb?: boolean — secretary sets it when question requires live/current data
Secretary never drafts stale answers: secretaryDraft suppressed whenever needsWeb is true
Coordinator receives system hint '[Live data required — call web_search before answering]' when needsWeb is true
classify prompt updated: needs_web field documented with clear examples (prices, weather, news, scores)
6 new tests covering: needsWeb=true detection, draft suppression, draft preserved when needsWeb=false, field omission default, prompt contains needs_web instruction

0.1.4.title: Web access, scheduling, and proactive memory
notes: web_search: searches the live web via Jina AI (s.jina.ai) — no API key required, returns clean markdown results
fetch_url: reads any URL as clean text via Jina AI reader (r.jina.ai) with direct fetch fallback
schedule_task: create once/interval/cron tasks via IPC — runs as new conversations in this chat at scheduled time
schedule_task supports: create, cancel, pause, resume, list — full task lifecycle without leaving chat
Memory system prompt rewritten: specific triggers (corrections, preferences, tool surprises, project shifts) — no longer waits to be asked
System capabilities updated: web and scheduling sections added to both system prompt and help menu

0.1.3.title: Secretary feedback loop — coordinator silently grades and calibrates routing quality
notes: gradeSecretaryResponse(): coordinator grades secretary's routing and draft after every response — fire-and-forget, zero user latency
Grades stored in /workspace/group/.secretary-feedback.json (per-group, rolling last 50 non-correct entries)
Only stores actionable feedback — correct grades discarded; suboptimal/wrong routing and inaccurate drafts logged
classifyMessage() injects last 5 corrections into secretary's classify prompt — secretary self-calibrates over time
usedSecretary flag: grading only fires when secretary actually ran (not regex fallback, not image shortcut)
Grade dimensions: routing (correct/suboptimal/wrong) + draft quality (accurate/incomplete/misleading)
Grader model: qwen3.5:35b (coordinator) — grades from the perspective of the model that responded

0.1.2.title: Test suite — routing, classification, escalation, and secretary review
notes: classify.test.ts: 47 tests covering the full routing and classification layer
extractContent, TASK_TEMPERATURE, selectModelFallback, shouldThinkFallback, detectTaskType — all pure-function tests with no mocking
getEscalationTier: full 4-step ladder tested (coder→analyst→architect→null)
classifyMessage: 13 cases — all model mappings, pre-escalation, secretary draft, JSON unwrapping, all 3 fallback triggers, temperature per task type
callOllama auto-escalation: failure phrase triggers next tier; empathy phrases don't; max tier stays put
callOllama secretary review: hint injected as system message and visible in coordinator's message array
handleToolCall: set_status and get_help coverage added
Scalability rule: new model → routing case; new task type → temperature loop + detectTaskType; new tool → describe block in index.test.ts

0.1.1.title: Secretary draft + coordinator review (boss listening from outside the door)
notes: Secretary (qwen3:4b) now drafts an answer for low-complexity messages alongside classifying them
Draft injected as a system message hint: coordinator echoes it verbatim if accurate, answers directly if not
Analogy: boss listening from outside the door — secretary handles the call, boss steps in only if needed
High-complexity messages still route straight to coordinator with no draft
Draft tracked in logs: [secretary drafted] suffix when draft is produced
Coordinator still uses keep_alive:-1; secretary draft call shares the same 8s timeout as classification

0.1.0.title: Secretary-as-classifier — semantic routing replaces regex
notes: classifyMessage(): every message now routed by qwen3:4b before inference — semantic understanding replaces keyword regex
Returns: model, think, complexity, task_type — 7-value enum (chat/code/creative/analysis/decision/debug/research)
complexity: 'high' pre-escalates default→analyst tier instead of waiting for failure
Regex classifiers kept as fallback — 8s timeout, graceful degradation if Secretary unavailable
responseType label now shows decision and research in status indicator
Coordinator (qwen3.5:35b) warmed at startup and pinned (keep_alive:-1); all specialists evict after 60s
generate_art + generate_film: qwen2.5vl:72b as end-to-end Artist/Cinematographer
llama3.2-vision and qwen3.5:27b removed (~25GB freed); all vision routed to qwen2.5vl:72b
Video timeout fixed (6min outer > 5min inner); enhancePrompt fixed (was calling deleted model)
Jarvis identity: team lead and generalist — default to doing it yourself, use the right specialist when they're the clear fit

0.0.69.title: Artist & Cinematographer — end-to-end visual generation
notes: generate_art tool: qwen2.5vl:72b acts as Artist — sees reference images directly, crafts an expert Flux prompt, selects backend, executes generation, returns optional recommendation note
generate_film tool: qwen2.5vl:72b acts as Cinematographer — same end-to-end pattern for video with cinematic prompt crafting
Artist/Cinematographer can recommend better tools via the note field (e.g. 'this would work better as a video')
Vision routing updated: selectModel() routes image messages to qwen2.5vl:72b (was llama3.2-vision)
describeImages() and describeImageVision() now use qwen2.5vl:72b for all reference image descriptions
MODEL_ALIASES updated: 'llama'/'vision'/'artist' all resolve to qwen2.5vl:72b
embellish on generate_art/generate_film is handled by the Artist — no separate enhancement hop needed

0.0.67.title: Auto-escalation on failure + expanded context windows
notes: Auto-escalation: if a model apologizes, fails, or receives an error it can't resolve, it automatically retries at the next reasoning tier
Triggers on: 'I apologize', 'my apologies', 'I wasn't able to', 'I couldn't complete', 'I failed to', 'I'm stuck', 'I don't know how to fix this'
Context windows expanded: 35b+think → 64k, deepseek-r1 → 64k, coder → 64k (was 32k)
Fast tier (35b no-think) stays at 16k for speed
'learn', 'teach', 'try' now trigger thinking mode

0.0.66.title: Sub-agent delegation + build failure alerting
notes: start.sh now tracks container build status in .build-status.json
Jarvis reports a warning immediately on startup if the container build failed at last launch
ollama_generate tool description now documents 4 sub-agent delegation patterns
System prompt updated: two explicit paths for bringing in a specialist — escalate (full handoff) vs ollama_generate (targeted consult, stays orchestrating)
Prevents silent stale-image failures where Jarvis never knew about broken builds

0.0.65.title: Deepseek-R1 escalation ladder + solutions architect persona
notes: Full 4-tier escalation ladder: qwen3.5:35b → qwen3.5:35b+think → deepseek-r1:32b → deepseek-r1:70b
DeepSeek-R1 models handled natively: no think flag needed, <think> tags extracted from response
Accuracy-optimized sampling profile for deepseek-r1 (top_k:50, top_p:0.95, temp:0.1)
Jarvis persona reframed: senior solutions architect managing a team of specialist models
Each team member has defined responsibilities: orchestrator, coder, analyst, strategist, architect
Status labels now unique per tier: Responding · Working · Thinking · Reasoning

0.0.46.title: Tier escalation
notes: Any model can now escalate to the next reasoning tier via the 'escalate' tool
Tier ladder: qwen3:4b → qwen3.5:35b → qwen3.5:35b+thinking (max)
Coder path: qwen3-coder:30b → qwen3.5:35b+thinking
At max tier: escalation is a no-op, model continues with best available capability
Replaces delegate_to_27b (backward compatible — old name still handled)

0.0.45.title: Broader thinking triggers
notes: THINK_PATTERN now triggers on: think, thinking, sure, determine, compare — standalone word matches
'Are you sure?', 'compare X and Y', 'determine which' all now activate thinking mode

0.0.43.title: Tighter thinking mode triggers
notes: DECISION_PATTERN trimmed — thinking no longer fires on 'recommend', 'what should', 'which is better', 'best way' (too casual)
Thinking now only triggers on hard decisions: 'should I/we', 'help me decide/choose/figure out', 'pros and cons', 'trade-offs'

0.0.42.title: Smarter coding model routing
notes: Coding model (qwen3-coder:30b) now routes on code as input or output — backticks in message, unambiguous coding verbs (implement/refactor/debug), or action verb targeting a code artifact (function, class, script, API, SQL, regex, etc.)
Old pattern matched too broadly — 'fix the grammar', 'write a poem' would incorrectly route to the coder model

0.0.40.title: Effective compression
notes: History compression fires at 20 messages (not 100) — keeps context tight while still using full message content for higher-quality summaries
Keeps last 8 messages verbatim; compresses everything older into a summary

0.0.39.title: No limits (local is free)
notes: Message history no longer truncated — full content preserved, no 400-char cap
History window raised to 200 messages, compression threshold to 100
Compression summary prompt no longer truncates messages at 300 chars
Context window unified at 32768 tokens across all models — no more per-tier caps

0.0.38.title: The 'I Know What I'm Doing' Update
notes: Thinking/loading indicators now show the model name instead of ellipses — you always know what's running
Routing table optimized: short analysis questions ('what is X') and brainstorm requests now route to qwen3:4b instead of 35b
Long-form creative writing still routes to 35b; explicit thinking/decision requests unchanged
qwen3-coder:30b now pre-warmed at startup — first coding response is instant
Context windows explicitly set: 8192 for 4b, 4096 for vision, 32768 for 35b/30b/27b — no more silent truncation on long conversations
Tighter sampling on fast tier (top_k=20, top_p=0.85) for more focused, slightly lower-latency responses

0.0.37.title: 100ms IPC polling
notes: IPC poll interval reduced to 100ms on both container and host — thinking updates, responses, and mid-task replies all reach Telegram ~10x faster

0.0.36.title: The 'Stop Making Me Wait' Update
notes: Thinking mode now only activates when you explicitly ask for it ('think harder', 'step by step') or for complex decisions — not for every explanation or creative request
Fast-tier threshold bumped from 15 to 25 words — more short messages get instant qwen3:4b responses
History compression now runs in the background while waiting for your next message, not blocking inference
Compression uses the already-warm qwen3.5:35b instead of cold-starting qwen3:32b
Model warmup at startup now runs in parallel — both models load simultaneously
IPC polling tightened from 500ms to 300ms for faster message delivery
Mid-task chat poller skips the first 2s — fast responses don't pay any polling overhead

0.0.35.title: The 'Don't Make Me Wait' Update
notes: You can now chat with Jarvis while he's working — send a message mid-task and get an immediate status update
Mid-task messages get a quick response from qwen3:4b with context about what's running and how long it's been going
Jarvis knows which tool is active (generating video, running command, etc.) and mentions it in his status reply
All mid-task exchanges are injected into history so Jarvis has full context when he wraps up

0.0.14.title: The Speedy Gonzales Update
notes: Deploy now shows exactly how long each step takes, so you can see where all those precious seconds are going
Response time is now tracked per message — model used, task type, thinking on/off, all logged for science
Warmup calls now fire in parallel and don't block startup, so the service is ready faster
Jarvis now sends exactly one 'I'm ready!' message per deploy — no more double-hellos

0.0.13.title: The 'Are We There Yet?' Update
notes: Deploys now tell you exactly how long each step took — container build, restart, everything
Fixed a sneaky bug where the deploy declared success before Jarvis actually woke up
Video model list now shows up in the startup message if they're already loaded — no more 'Loading...' followed by another message

0.0.12.title: The 'Stop Following Me' Update
notes: Jarvis now wakes up with a single clean message — the 'starting...' and 'ready!' were getting redundant
Video backends that are already running show up right in the startup message instead of trickling in later
Deploy waits for Jarvis to actually say hello before declaring victory — fixed a timing issue where it was celebrating too early

0.0.11.title: The 'Fast and Furious' Update
notes: Container builds are now dramatically faster — npm packages and system deps are cached between deploys, only your code gets rebuilt
Deploys now run in the background — trigger one and keep chatting while it works
If a new deploy starts while the old one is still going, the old one gets politely cancelled (with a notification)
Version numbers now follow proper semver (0.0.x) and only bump if the deploy actually succeeds

0.0.10.title: The 'Hold On, Let Me Think' Update
notes: Thinking mode is now off by default — turns on automatically when you ask hard questions or say 'think harder'
When thinking is active, you see the full reasoning chain, not just the final answer
The 'I'm working on it' indicator now says 'Thinking...', 'Lagging...', or 'Processing...' depending on what's actually happening
Video generation startup no longer freezes Jarvis — it checks backends in the background and stays responsive

0.0.9.title: The 'Say That Again?' Update
notes: Voice memos now get transcribed locally — no API key required, all on-device
Jarvis reads back the transcript before responding so you can catch any transcription goofs
You can now say 'stop', 'cancel', or 'nevermind' to cut off a long-running response mid-flight
Cancelling also stops ComfyUI if it's generating a video in the background
